title: Identify centers or boundaries of some region of interest
text: common task spatial analysis perform analysis region interest, tumor. vignette, demonstrate use NeighborhoodCenters() function help us identify characteristics center region interest boundary.

title: Identify centers or boundaries of some region of interest
text: use 10X Visium dataset, downloaded GSE208253, demonstrate NeighborhoodCenters().
code: scc_s1 = UpdateSeuratObject(scc_s1) #> Validating object structure #> Updating object slots #> Ensuring keys are in the proper structure #> Ensuring keys are in the proper structure #> Ensuring feature names don't have underscores or pipes #> Updating slots in Spatial #> Updating slots in slice1 #> Warning: Not validating Centroids objects #> Updated Centroids object 'centroids' in FOV 'slice1' #> Updated boundaries in FOV 'slice1' #> Validating object structure for Assay5 'Spatial' #> Validating object structure for VisiumV2 'slice1' #> Object representation is consistent with the most current Seurat version class(scc_s1) #> [1] "Seurat" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SeuratObject" dim(scc_s1) #> [1] 36601  1185  coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')]

title: Identify centers or boundaries of some region of interest
text: vignette, use clustering select region interest tissue sample, practice, instead region biological interest. region interest, .e., neighborhood, provided NeighborhoodCenters() named vector TRUE/FALSE values, names correspond spot barcodes given dataset.
code: # create an igraph object with SpotGraph() and perform clustering ig = SpotGraph(coord) cl = igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(ig) cl = factor(cl$membership)  # select cluster 1 and name the boolean vector with vertex names roi = cl==1 names(roi) = names(igraph::V(ig))

title: Identify centers or boundaries of some region of interest
text: output function returns list, first element data.frame scores igraph::centr_eigen() is_neighborhood == T, 0 is_neighborhood == F.
code: res = NeighborhoodCenters(coord = coord, is_neighborhood = roi) print(head(res$eigen.scores)) #>              barcode center_eigen #> 1 AAACACCAATAACTGC-1 0.0000000000 #> 2 AAACAGGGTCTATATT-1 0.0006409795 #> 3 AAACCGTTCGTCCAGG-1 0.0000000000 #> 4 AAACGAGACGGTTGAT-1 0.0000000000 #> 5 AAACTGCTGGCTCCAA-1 0.0000000000 #> 6 AAAGACTGGGCGCTTT-1 0.0000000000  # Set centrality scores as a vertex attribute in our igraph object igraph::V(ig)$centr_eigen = res$eigen.scores$center_eigen igraph::V(ig)$roi_center = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$centers igraph::V(ig)$roi_boundary = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$boundary  plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'centr_eigen',                         pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_viridis() +   theme_void() plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_center',                         pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = 'none') plt3 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_boundary',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void()  wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3, design = 'A#\nBC')

title: Identify centers or boundaries of some region of interest
text: NeighborhoodCenters() function can also identify characteristics multiple regions providing named boolean vector indicating whether spot within regions. can useful several tumor regions within tissue section. , select three different clusters regions interest, instead just one.
code: roi = cl %in% c('1', '2', '5') names(roi) = names(igraph::V(ig)) res = NeighborhoodCenters(coord = coord, is_neighborhood = roi) # Set centrality scores as a vertex attribute in our igraph object igraph::V(ig)$centr_eigen = res$eigen.scores$center_eigen igraph::V(ig)$roi_center = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$centers igraph::V(ig)$roi_boundary = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$boundary  plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'centr_eigen',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_viridis() +   theme_void() plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_center',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = 'none') plt3 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_boundary',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void()  wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3, design = 'A#\nBC')

what: 5. Calculate distance between each spot and ROI center
title: Identify centers or boundaries of some region of interest
code: roi_dist = igraph::distances(ig, weights = NA)[,res$centers] %>%    apply(1, min) boundary_dist = igraph::distances(ig, weights = NA)[,res$boundary] %>%    apply(1, min) # boundary_dist = ifelse(!roi, boundary_dist, 0)  igraph::V(ig)$roi_dist = roi_dist igraph::V(ig)$boundary_dist = boundary_dist  plt.dist = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_dist',                  pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_distiller(palette = 'RdBu', direction = 1) +   ggtitle('distance to ROI centers') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none') plt.boundarydist = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'boundary_dist',                  pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_distiller(palette = 'RdBu', direction = 1) +   ggtitle('distance to ROI boundaries') +   guides(color = guide_colorbar(title = 'distance'),          alpha = guide_none()) +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'))  wrap_plots(plt.dist, plt.boundarydist, nrow = 1)

title: QuickStart
code: # retrieve data from TENxVisium eh = ExperimentHub::ExperimentHub() q = AnnotationHub::query(eh, 'TENxVisium') id = q$ah_id[q$title=='HumanGlioblastoma_v3.13'] spe = eh[[id]]

title: QuickStart
code: # 1. prepare CleanSlide() inputs coord.xy = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe) %>% as.data.frame() ncount = colSums(assay(spe))  # 2. run CleanSlide() res = CleanSlide(coord = coord.xy, nCount = ncount)  # 3. store results in SpatialExperiment object colData(spe) = cbind(colData(spe), res)  # 4. apply filter automatically determined by threshold calculation spe = spe[,spe$threshold]

title: SpotGraphs
text: demonstrate general usage SpotGraph, use 10X Visium dataset squamous cell carcinoma patient, downloaded GSE208253. raw data GEO provided Seurat object package use example.
code: scc_s1 = Seurat::UpdateSeuratObject(scc_s1) #> Validating object structure #> Updating object slots #> Ensuring keys are in the proper structure #> Ensuring keys are in the proper structure #> Ensuring feature names don't have underscores or pipes #> Updating slots in Spatial #> Updating slots in slice1 #> Warning: Not validating Centroids objects #> Updated Centroids object 'centroids' in FOV 'slice1' #> Updated boundaries in FOV 'slice1' #> Validating object structure for Assay5 'Spatial' #> Validating object structure for VisiumV2 'slice1' #> Object representation is consistent with the most current Seurat version class(scc_s1) #> [1] "Seurat" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SeuratObject" dim(scc_s1) #> [1] 36601  1185

title: SpotGraphs
text: goal package support Visium analysis providing function constructs igraph object based number immediately adjacent spots slide. opens analysis pipeline various igraph functions perform certain tasks : identifying spots lying tissue debris removed downstream analysis distance calculations spots measuring shortest path network spot easily identifying edges pre-defined regions tissue construct igraph object, need x,y coordinates spots slide. can plot igraph object ggnetwork package, interfaces directly ggplot2, use SpatialPlotGraph function provided package visualize edges drawn node original x,y coordinates stored igraph object. Note is_boundary attribute automatically calculated running SpotGraph(), use color node plots.
code: coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')] head(coord) #>                        x     y #> AAACACCAATAACTGC-1  4809 16571 #> AAACAGGGTCTATATT-1  3944 13546 #> AAACCGTTCGTCCAGG-1  8142 14812 #> AAACGAGACGGTTGAT-1 13505 10536 #> AAACTGCTGGCTCCAA-1 11764 13053 #> AAAGACTGGGCGCTTT-1  4240  9011  ig = SpotGraph(coord = coord) ig #> IGRAPH 2291b2d UN-- 1185 3189 --  #> + attr: name (v/c), coord_x (v/n), coord_y (v/n), is_boundary (v/l) #> + edges from 2291b2d (vertex names): #>  [1] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--AGGCGGTTTGTCCCGC-1 #>  [2] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--CTCGTCGAGGGCTCAT-1 #>  [3] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--GAAACATAGGAAACAG-1 #>  [4] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--GGAACCTTGACTCTGC-1 #>  [5] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--TCCCTGGCGTATTAAC-1 #>  [6] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--TGGACGCAATCCAGCC-1 #>  [7] AAACAGGGTCTATATT-1--ACAGTAATACAACTTG-1 #>  [8] AAACAGGGTCTATATT-1--TTCCCGGCGCCAATAG-1 #> + ... omitted several edges plt.ggnet = ggplot(ig, aes(x=x, y=y, xend=xend, yend=yend)) +   geom_edges() +    geom_nodes(aes(color = is_boundary), size = 0.5) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = 'none')  plt.spg = SpatialPlotGraph(igraph_object = ig,                             group.by = 'is_boundary',                             flip.axes = T,                             pt.size = 0.5)  patchwork::wrap_plots(plt.ggnet, plt.spg)

title: SpotGraphs
text: One main features SpotGraph package identify spots slide lie top tissue debris disconnected rest tissue sample. uninformative technical artifact sample processing, ’d like exclude downstream analysis. CleanSlide() function provides simple way automatically identify spots, based connectivity spots number transcripts detected within community spots. First, extract two inputs required CleanSlide() function: x,y coordinates spot dataset vector transcript counts detected spot output CleanSlide() data.frame number rows input coordinate data.frame matrix can directly used Seurat::AddMetaData() re-insert back original Seurat object.  can now observe results CleanSlide() Seurat::SpatialDimPlot()  can agree results directly apply threshold filter spots subset(scc_s1, threshold), can manually identify groups spots want filter . ‘ig_cluster’ metadata column stores communities spots, spot immediately adjacent spots.  case, manually remove clusters 9, 10, 23, 24, 26, 22, 15, 18, 19, 17, 20, 25.
code: coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')] nCounts = scc_s1$nCount_Spatial res = CleanSlide(coord, nCount = nCounts) scc_s1 = Seurat::AddMetaData(scc_s1, res) Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(scc_s1, group.by = 'threshold', image.alpha = 0.6) Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(scc_s1, group.by = 'ig_cluster', image.alpha = 0.6, label = T, label.size = 3) spot_clusters_to_remove = c(9, 10, 23, 24, 26, 22, 15, 18, 19, 17, 20, 25) scc_s1 = scc_s1[, !scc_s1$ig_cluster %in% spot_clusters_to_remove] Seurat::SpatialFeaturePlot(scc_s1, features = 'nCount_Spatial', pt.size.factor = 2)

title: SpotGraphs
text: working igraph object, may desirable remove edges two groups spots.
code: # Create igraph object coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')] ig = SpotGraph(coord)  # Clustering with igraph igraph::V(ig)$clusters = factor(igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(ig)$membership) plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'clusters', label = T) +   ggtitle('before') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none')  # Remove edges between pairs of clusters ig = CutEdges(ig, cluster_pairs = list(c(2,1), c(2,10)), cluster.col = 'clusters') plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'clusters', label = T) +   ggtitle('after') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none')  plt3 = plt2 +    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.35,0.8), ylim = c(0.4, 1)) +   ggtitle('after/zoomed') patchwork::wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3)

what: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
title: ThresholdTests
code: library(SpotGraphs) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(ggplot2) library(viridis) library(patchwork)

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
what: Load several datasets to get a survey of potential cluster_nCount distributions
title: ThresholdTests
code: # retrieve data from TENxVisium eh = ExperimentHub::ExperimentHub() q = AnnotationHub::query(eh, 'TENxVisium') ds.names = c('HumanLymphNode',               'MouseBrainCoronal',              'HumanColorectalCancer_v3.13',              'HumanSpinalCord_v3.13',              'HumanGlioblastoma_v3.13',              'MouseKidneyCoronal_v3.13') id = q$ah_id[q$title %in% ds.names] spe.ls = lapply(id, function(ds.id) eh[[ds.id]]) names(spe.ls) = ds.names %>% str_replace('\\.', '_')

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
title: ThresholdTests
code: spe.ls = lapply(spe.ls, function(spe) {   colData(spe)$nCount = colSums(assay(spe))   return(spe) }) res.ls = lapply(spe.ls, function(spe) {   coord = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe) %>% as.data.frame()   colnames(coord) = c('x', 'y')   CleanSlide(coord = coord, nCount = spe$nCount) })

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
what: Store the result dataframe in each SpatialExperiment object
title: ThresholdTests
code: for (ds.name in names(res.ls)) {   colData(spe.ls[[ds.name]]) = cbind(colData(spe.ls[[ds.name]]), res.ls[[ds.name]]) }

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
what: Define a function to plot nCounts on tissue image
title: ThresholdTests
code: ImageFeature = function(obj, feature = 'nCount', label = F) {   # First, scale the x,y coordinates to fit the low-res H&E image   lowres_scale <- imgData(obj)[imgData(obj)$image_id == 'lowres', 'scaleFactor']   coord.xy = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(obj) %>% as.data.frame()   coord.xy$x_axis <- coord.xy$pxl_row_in_fullres * lowres_scale   coord.xy$y_axis <- coord.xy$pxl_col_in_fullres * lowres_scale        # Flip the y-axis   coord.xy$y_axis <- abs(coord.xy$y_axis - (ncol(imgRaster(obj)) + 1))        # Get metadata feature from object   coord.xy[,feature] = colData(obj)[,feature]    # Create plot of transcripts per spot overlaid ontop of H&E   plt = ggplot(mapping = aes(1:600, 1:600)) +     annotation_raster(imgRaster(obj),                        xmin = 1, xmax = 600,                        ymin = 1, ymax = 600) +     geom_point(data=coord.xy, alpha=0.5, size = 0.5,                aes_string(x='x_axis', y='y_axis', color=feature)) +     scale_color_viridis(option = 'turbo', name = feature) +     coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1,600),                      ylim = c(1,600)) +     coord_fixed() +     theme_void()      if (label) {     df.labels = coord.xy %>%        reframe(.by = all_of(feature),                x_axis = mean(x_axis),               y_axis = mean(y_axis))          plt = plt +       geom_label(data = df.labels,                   size = 3,                  aes_string(x='x_axis',                             y='y_axis',                              label = feature,                              color=feature))   }   return(plt) }

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
what: Plot spotgraph cluster nCounts on tissue images
title: ThresholdTests
code: plt.ncount = lapply(names(spe.ls), function(ds.name) {   ImageFeature(spe.ls[[ds.name]], feature = 'cluster_nCount', label = T) +     ggtitle(ds.name) +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),           legend.position = 'none',           plot.margin = margin(rep(0.75,4),'cm')) })  wrap_plots(plt.ncount, ncol = 2)

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
title: ThresholdTests
code: # get count densities of each slide den.ls = lapply(res.ls, function(res) {   nCount = unique(res$cluster_nCount)   den = density(log10(nCount))   return(den) })  # calculate different thresholds for each slide plt.tests = list() thres.perc.ls = list() thres.fixed.ls = list()  for (ds.name in names(den.ls)) {   den = den.ls[[ds.name]]      # quantile window   int.perc = c(spatstat.univar::quantile.density(den, 0.05),                 spatstat.univar::quantile.density(den, 0.75))   thres.perc.ls[[ds.name]] = approxfun(den$x, den$y) %>% optimise(interval=int.perc)   thres.perc.ls[[ds.name]] = 10^(thres.perc.ls[[ds.name]]$minimum)      # fixed window   int.fixed = c(quantile(den$x, 0.05), quantile(den$x, 0.75))   thres.fixed.ls[[ds.name]] = approxfun(den$x, den$y) %>% optimise(interval=int.fixed)   thres.fixed.ls[[ds.name]] = 10^(thres.fixed.ls[[ds.name]]$minimum)      # create data frame for plotting   df = data.frame(x = den$x, y = den$y)      # create plots   plt.base = ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +     geom_line() +     theme_bw() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))      plt.perc = plt.base +      geom_vline(xintercept = int.perc, color = 'red') +     geom_vline(xintercept = log10(thres.perc.ls[[ds.name]]), color = 'blue') +     ylab(ds.name) +     ggtitle(       label = paste('thres w/ quantiles int:',                      round(thres.perc.ls[[ds.name]], 0))       )   plt.fixed = plt.base +      geom_vline(xintercept = int.fixed, color = 'red') +     geom_vline(xintercept = log10(thres.fixed.ls[[ds.name]]), color = 'blue') +     ggtitle(       label = paste('thres w/ fixed int:',                      round(thres.fixed.ls[[ds.name]], 0))       ) +     theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())      plt.tests[[ds.name]] = wrap_plots(plt.perc, plt.fixed)      # clean up variables that change every loop   rm(den, int.perc, int.fixed, df, plt.base, plt.perc, plt.fixed) }  wrap_plots(plt.tests, ncol = 1)

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
what: Plot which spots would be filtered out with each threshold
title: ThresholdTests
code: # add thresholds to each SpatialExperiment object for (ds.name in names(spe.ls)) {   thres.perc = spe.ls[[ds.name]]$cluster_nCount > thres.perc.ls[[ds.name]]   thres.fixed = spe.ls[[ds.name]]$cluster_nCount > thres.fixed.ls[[ds.name]]   spe.ls[[ds.name]]$threshold.perc = thres.perc %>%      ifelse('pass', 'nopass') %>%      factor(levels = c('nopass', 'pass'))   spe.ls[[ds.name]]$threshold.fixed = thres.fixed %>%      ifelse('pass', 'nopass') %>%      factor(levels = c('nopass', 'pass'))   rm(thres.perc, thres.fixed) }  # create plots plt.thresholds = lapply(names(spe.ls), function(ds.name) {   plt1 = ImageFeature(spe.ls[[ds.name]], feature = 'threshold.perc', label = F) +     scale_color_manual(values = c('red', 'grey'), drop = F) +     ggtitle(ds.name, subtitle = 'percentiles') +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),           plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),           legend.position = 'none',            plot.margin = margin(rep(0.75,4),'cm'))   plt2 = ImageFeature(spe.ls[[ds.name]], feature = 'threshold.fixed', label = F) +     scale_color_manual(values = c('red', 'grey'), drop = F) +     ggtitle(ds.name, subtitle = 'fixed window') +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),           plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),           legend.position = 'none',            plot.margin = margin(rep(0.75,4),'cm'))   return(wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, ncol = 2)) })  wrap_plots(plt.thresholds, ncol = 1)

previous_headings: Test different strategies to automatically detect a threshold for filtering
what: Use eccentricity to determine which spots should be filtered out
title: ThresholdTests
text: eccentricity = max distance one spot spots
code: # Create igraph objects for each SPE object ig.ls = lapply(spe.ls, function(spe) {   coord = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe) %>% as.data.frame()   colnames(coord) = c('x', 'y')   SpotGraph(coord = coord) })  # Calculate eccentricity of all points on each slide for (id in names(ig.ls)) {   igraph::V(ig.ls[[id]])$ecc = igraph::eccentricity(ig.ls[[id]], weights = NA) }  # Plot eccentricity plt.ecc = lapply(names(ig.ls), function(id) {   SpatialPlotGraph(ig.ls[[id]], group.by = 'ecc') +     scale_color_viridis() +     ggtitle(id) +     guides(alpha = guide_none(),            color = guide_colorbar(title = 'ecc')) +     theme_void() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold')) }) wrap_plots(plt.ecc, ncol = 2) plt.eccthres = lapply(names(ig.ls), function(id) {   ig = ig.ls[[id]]   thres = 5   igraph::V(ig)$ecc_thres = ifelse(igraph::V(ig)$ecc > thres, 'pass', 'nopass') %>%      factor(levels = c('nopass', 'pass'))      spe = spe.ls[[id]]   spe$ecc_thres = igraph::V(ig)$ecc_thres      ImageFeature(spe, feature = 'ecc_thres') +     scale_color_manual(values = c('red', 'grey'), drop = F) +     ggtitle(id) +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),           legend.position = 'none',            plot.margin = margin(rep(0.75,4),'cm')) }) #> Warning: In `margin()`, the argument `t` should have length 1, not length 4. #> ℹ Argument get(s) truncated to length 1. #> Warning in unit(c(t, r, b, l), unit): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning: In `margin()`, the argument `t` should have length 1, not title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: create igraph object given 2-column data frame matrix x,y coordinates node = spot, edge exists two spots adjacent returns igraph object takes 2-column data frame matrix x,y coordinates calls SpotGraph create igraph object performs modularity maximization group connected communities spots adds total transcripts together per-cluster (nCount) calculates threshold identify low quality/small communities spots returns data frame per-spot cluster results, total counts per cluster, whether cluster passed automatically detected threshold use original x,y coordinates plot vertex_attr corresponding igraph object remove edges specified groups spots igraph object

what: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
text: Identify spots 10X Visium Seurat object potentially low quality. Mostly useful slides large areas without tissue standard spot-filtering based nCounts insufficient. Identify neighboring spots x,y coordinates SpotGraph() Performing modularity maximization identify clusters Calculate total number transcripts detected cluster (nCount) Set threshold identify clusters filtered

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
code: CleanSlide(coord, nCount)

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. nCount named vector transcript counts per spot, names spot barcodes/ids, element match row coord.

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
text: data.frame following columns: ig_cluster: igraph modularity maximization results cluster_nCount: summed transcript counts within 'ig_cluster' group threshold: whether respective 'ig_cluster' group passed automatically determiend threshold barcode: spot id barcode sequence

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame with two isolated groups of points df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y') nspots = dim(df)[1] rownames(df) = paste0('spot', 1:nspots)  # Create some vector of transcript counts spotcounts = rbinom(25, 10000, 0.3) spotcounts = c(spotcounts, rbinom(9, 100, 0.3)) names(spotcounts) = rownames(df)  # The resulting data.frame should indicate that the smaller # cluster of 3x3 points did not pass the threshold, while the # larger group of 5x5 points should have passed the threshold. res = CleanSlide(coord = df, nCount = spotcounts) } # }

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
text: updated igraph object edges removed

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y')  # Create an igraph object with these coordinates ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Perform clustering and add those cluster assignments to the igraph object cl = igraph::cluster_louvain(ig)$membership ig = igraph::set_vertex_attr(ig, 'cluster', value = factor(cl))  # Examine graph before removing edges SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'cluster')  # Remove edges between clusters 1 and 2, and between clusters 3 and 4 ig = CutEdges(igraph_object = ig,               cluster_pairs = list(c(1,2), c(1,3)),               cluster.col = 'cluster')  # Examine graph after removing edges SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'cluster') } # }

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates, rownames spot barcodes/ids. is_neighborhood boolean vector (TRUE/FALSE) equal length number rows coord. Centers identified neighborhood spots labeled TRUE.

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
text: list following elements: eigen.scores: numeric vector output igraph::centr_eigen, score spot, max(eigen.scores) within neighborhood identify center. centers: character vector barcode ids spots identified center neighborhood spots. boundaries: character vector barcode ids spots identified boundary neighborhood spots.

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y') nspots = dim(df)[1] rownames(df) = paste0('spot', 1:nspots)  igraph::V(ig)$cluster = igraph::cluster_leiden(ig, resolution = 0)$membership is_cluster = igraph::V(ig)$cluster=='1' names(is_cluster) = names(igraph::V(ig))  res = NeighborhoodCenters(coord = df, is_neighborhood = is_cluster) } # }

Invert x y axes, typically needed coordinates Visium Seurat objects align spots tissue image. linewidth Numeric input determine width segments nodes. Used geom_segment(). pt.size Numeric input determine size point plot. Used size parameter geom_point().

what: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: Create igraph object x,y coordinates, assuming adjacent spots equidistant , drawing edges spots immediately adjacent .

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
code: SpotGraph(   coord,   delaunay = F,   delaunay.trim = T,   dist.buffer = 1.05,   max.dist = NULL )

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. delaunay Whether use Delaunay triangulation identify edges network construction. Default FALSE. delaunay.trim TRUE (default) delaunay = TRUE, edge lengths longer max.dist removed Delaunay triangulation. dist.buffer Influences maximum distance node allowed another node considered neighbor. used max.dist = NULL. max.dist furthest node another node considered neighbors. NULL (default), shortest distance two nodes minimum distance (min.dist) required two nodes considered neighbors, max.dist calculated hypotenuse triangle two sides length min.dist, multiplied dist.buffer.

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: igraph object, vertex (.e., node) corresponds row coord input, un-weighted edges vertices immediately adjacent .

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame with two isolated groups of points df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y')  # Preview the points on a grid with their corresponding x,y coordinates ggplot(df, aes(x = x,y=y)) +   geom_point()  # Identify points that are immediately adjacent to each other and create an igraph object ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Optionally view the network with ggnetwork # library(ggnetwork) ggplot(ig, aes(x=x, y=y, xend=xend, yend=yend)) +   geom_edges() +   geom_nodes() } # }

title: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
